sources.notes: Read the path-deletion operation and constraints: one path, possibly a single vertex, is removed from a tree with total n at most 2e5.

The official English editorial revision and reference implementation confirm a linear DFS DP that keeps the best one-arm value and combines the two best child arms; the prose widget itself was dynamically unavailable.
The Luogu solution independently uses tree DP states for the removed path avoiding a node, ending at it, or passing through it, and combines the two best child contributions.

Derived components(P)=2+sum_{v in P}(deg(v)-2), then compared the resulting maximum-weight-path DP against explicit removal of every endpoint path on 1000 random trees for each n=2..10; all cases matched.

annotation.summary: Choose one simple path in a tree, delete all its vertices, and maximize the number of connected components left behind.
annotation.constraints: 2 <= n <= 2e5 per test and total n <= 2e5, so the answer must be computed in O(n) or O(n log n).
annotation.core_idea: For a removed path P, every remaining component is attached by exactly one edge from P to outside P. Thus the component count is sum_{v in P} deg(v)-2(|P|-1)=2+sum_{v in P}(deg(v)-2). Assign vertex weight w(v)=deg(v)-2 and find a maximum-weight simple path. Root the tree; down[v]=w(v)+max(0,max down[child]), while the best path through v combines w(v) with the two largest positive child down-values. Add 2 to the global maximum path sum.
annotation.complexity: O(n) time and O(n) memory per test case.
tricks: Count surviving components by off-path edges.
Rewrite the objective as an additive vertex weight deg(v)-2.
Combine the two best positive downward arms at each vertex.

solution_variants.name: degree-weighted-maximum-path-dp
solution_variants.summary: Transform every vertex to weight deg(v)-2, then run a diameter-style tree DP that keeps the best downward arm and combines the top two child arms.

solution_variants.name: endpoint-middle-component-dp
solution_variants.summary: Directly store the best component count for a deleted path ending at the current vertex and for a path passing through it, combining one or two child states as in the official and Luogu formulations.

tags.evidence: After the degree-weight reduction, the algorithm is the standard top-two-arms tree DP for a maximum-weight simple path.
tags.description: Tree dynamic programming that maximizes an additive score over one simple path by storing the best downward arm at each vertex and combining the two best child arms.
tags.created_reason: The existing dp/tree-dp tag is too broad to retrieve the recurring maximum-path or diameter-style top-two-child recurrence directly.

tags.evidence: The proof that off-path branches become distinct components and the linear parent-child recurrence both depend essentially on the input being a tree.
